affected_files.purpose: TestGraphRoundTrip: full insert/compaction/traversal round-trip correctness test with an independent serial oracle, two pre-restart append+compact cycles, a simulated process restart (fresh LoadCSR), and a third post-restart append+compact cycle

dependents.note: Pure test-only addition; no package outside engine/graph imports test files. No production API surface changes, so no dependent packages (engine/split, engine/wal, future agents/ingestion, agents/query) are affected.

risk_rationale: Test-only change, additive new file, zero production code modified. Primary risk is a flaky or incorrect oracle giving false confidence, mitigated by computing the oracle independently (plain Go map iteration mirroring documented merge/traversal semantics) rather than calling package-under-test merge/traversal logic, and by deliberately targeting the two previously-confirmed real bug classes (compaction retry double-counting, WAL segment-number reuse after truncation) across multiple append+compact cycles plus a simulated restart.
concurrency_note: Test is intentionally single-goroutine/sequential (issue's own test spec says 'interleave inserts/compaction/traversal', i.e. serial ordering, not concurrent execution) but still run under -race per package convention since it exercises EdgeLog's internal RWMutex/wal.Writer machinery.
durability_note: Includes a simulated process-restart check (fresh LoadCSR against the same graph.dat path, discarding the in-memory CSRGraph Compact returned) to prove on-disk durability, not just in-memory correctness within one process lifetime.
